narration: The longest common prefix keeps going only while every word still shares one next letter.
When the trie branches, the shared beginning stops because the words no longer all agree.
decision: One road means shared. Two roads mean split.

predictionPrompt.prompt: If the path after fl splits into o and i, what is the longest common prefix?

choices.label: fl
choices.feedback: Correct. The shared path stops before the split.

choices.label: flo
choices.feedback: Not this one. Not every word continues with o.

narration: A shorter whole word can also stop the answer, because the shared beginning cannot go past a finished word.
callout: If one word is to, the common prefix cannot go farther than to.

reflectionPrompt.prompt: Why does a branch point stop the longest common prefix?
